---
title: "Primrose-leaved Violet (Viola primulifolia)"
slug: "viola-primulifolia"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621865559/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Primrose-leaved Violet"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Malpighiales"
tax_family: "Violaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Malpighiales", "Violaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621865559/medium.jpg" alt="Primrose-leaved Violet" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/scarnevale">
scarnevale</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341583183">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Malpighiales
-   **Family:** Violaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=245813)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Rare across Florida.

## Natural history notes

A small wildflower characterized by its narrow, elongated leaves and delicate pale violet to white flowers with darker veins. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as bogs, wet flatwoods, and along stream margins. It typically blooms in spring and provides an early-season nectar source for small pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/245813-Viola-primulifolia)
-   [Local profile](https://explorer.natureserve.org/Taxon/ELEMENT_GLOBAL.2.962515/Viola_primulifolia)
